name: Red Skelton
abstract: Richard Bernard Skelton (July 18, 1913 – September 17, 1997) was an American entertainer best known for his national radio and television shows between 1937 and 1971, especially as host of the television program The Red Skelton Show. He has stars on the Hollywood Walk of Fame for his work in radio and television, and he also appeared in …

about_quotes.speaker: Luther Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: And also you work … so you worked with Red Skelton... [INTERVIEWEE] All the same... these constitute what I call gigs... You're a professional arranger, composer or whatever and you, what comes across your desk, you do it
about_quotes.doc_title: Luther Henderson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Hardaway
about_quotes.evidence: I did a lot of live television work. I did a lot during the 60s and 70s, there must have been eight or ten musical variety shows on the air. There was Dean Martin and Carol Burnett, Sonny & Cher, Jack Benny, Red Skelton
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Hardaway interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer lists 'Red Skelton' among various TV shows; the interviewee does not mention Red Skelton personally or describe a relationship.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nancy Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Coquille
about_quotes.evidence: Sally Corby would be in a Red Skelton chorus line when he would be performing in one of the big runs

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: I did a thing with Hazel Scott and Red Skelton called 'I Doed It'
